title: Tokenization Allows Multimodal Large Language Models to Understand, Generate and Edit Architectural Floor Plans
task: 建筑平面图理解、生成与局部编辑
inputData: RPLAN住宅平面图、建筑轮廓、自然语言空间描述与编辑指令
contribution: 提出HouseMind，将建筑轮廓和房间实例分别离散为可与语言共同建模的空间token，并在一个轻量多模态大语言模型中统一完成平面图理解、条件生成和指令编辑。
abstract: Architectural floor plan design demands joint reasoning over geometry, semantics, and spatial hierarchy, which remains a major challenge for current AI systems. Although recent diffusion and language models improve visual fidelity, they still struggle with coherent spatial reasoning and controllable generation. HouseMind is a multimodal large language model that unifies floor plan understanding, generation, and editing in one framework. It introduces discrete room-instance tokens to construct a unified vocabulary that bridges layouts and symbolic reasoning. With multimodal alignment and instruction tuning, the model synthesizes coherent, controllable layouts from text instructions while remaining efficient and locally deployable.
journal: Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR 2026)
methodology: HouseMind uses two lightweight VQ-VAE branches to tokenize the global building outline and each room instance conditioned on that outline. The resulting outline, room-label and room-geometry tokens are appended to the vocabulary of a Qwen3-0.6B backbone. Training has three full-parameter stages: spatial-token embedding initialization, autoregressive multimodal pre-training on interleaved text and spatial tokens, and supervised instruction tuning for layout understanding, text-and-outline-conditioned generation, and natural-language editing. Based on RPLAN, Qwen3-30B-A3B produces simple and detailed descriptions from canonical JSON records; the resulting benchmark contains 76,122 training, 2,308 validation and 2,308 shared test samples.
results: On the 2,308-sample test set, the task-specific understanding model achieved a room match rate of 0.998, location accuracy of 0.969, adjacency accuracy of 0.990 and relation accuracy of 0.808 in about 3 seconds per sample. The unified HouseMind-O generator reached Micro/Macro IoU of 0.710/0.654, FID 1.89, Node F1 0.994 and edge overlap 0.880 in about 2 seconds, compared with 0.589/0.521 IoU and FID 11.3 for ChatHouseDiffusion. The task-specific editor achieved change IoU 0.608, GED 0.467 and Node F1 0.998 in about 3 seconds. Current editing is limited mainly to room addition and deletion; doors, windows and furniture are not modeled, and outputs are not yet fully aligned with professional design preferences.
taskEn: Architectural floor plan understanding, generation, and localized editing
inputDataEn: RPLAN residential floor plans, building outlines, natural-language spatial descriptions, and editing instructions
